    <item>
  <title>Monday of the Fifth Week of Easter</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/050426.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading I  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/14?5 ">Acts 14:5-18</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span><span>There was an attempt in Iconium<br>
by both the Gentiles and the Jews,<br>
together with their leaders,<br>
to attack and stone Paul and Barnabas.<br>
They realized it,<br>
and fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe<br>
and to the surrounding countryside,<br>
where they continued to proclaim the Good News.<br>
<br>
At Lystra there was a crippled man, lame from birth,<br>
who had never walked.<br>
He listened to Paul speaking, who looked intently at him,<br>
saw that he had the faith to be healed,<br>
and called out in a loud voice, “Stand up straight on your feet.”<br>
He jumped up and began to walk about.<br>
When the crowds saw what Paul had done,<br>
they cried out in Lycaonian,<br>
“The gods have come down to us in human form.”<br>
They called Barnabas “Zeus” and Paul “Hermes,”<br>
because he was the chief speaker.<br>
And the priest of Zeus, whose temple was at the entrance to the city,<br>
brought oxen and garlands to the gates,<br>
for he together with the people intended to offer sacrifice.<br>
<br>
The Apostles Barnabas and Paul tore their garments<br>
when they heard this and rushed out into the crowd, shouting,<br>
“Men, why are you doing this?<br>
We are of the same nature as you, human beings.<br>
We proclaim to you good news<br>
that you should turn from these idols to the living God,<br>
<em>who made heaven and earth and sea and all that is in them.</em><br>
In past generations he allowed all Gentiles to go their own ways;<br>
yet, in bestowing his goodness,<br>
he did not leave himself without witness,<br>
for he gave you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons,<br>
and filled you with nourishment and gladness for your hearts.”<br>
Even with these words, they scarcely restrained the crowds<br>
from offering sacrifice to them.</span></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/115?1 ">Psalm 115:1-2, 3-4, 15-16</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; (1ab)&nbsp; <strong>Not to us, O Lord, but to your name give the glory.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>Not to us, O LORD, not to us</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; but to your name give glory</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; because of your mercy, because of your truth.</span><br><span>Why should the pagans say,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; “Where is their God?”</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Not to us, O Lord, but to your name give the glory.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>Our God is in heaven;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; whatever he wills, he does.</span><br><span>Their idols are silver and gold,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; the handiwork of men.</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Not to us, O Lord, but to your name give the glory.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>May you be blessed by the LORD,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; who made heaven and earth.</span><br><span>Heaven is the heaven of the LORD,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; but the earth he has given to the children of men.</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Not to us, O Lord, but to your name give the glory.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/14?26 ">John 14:26</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>The Holy Spirit will teach you everything</span><br><span>and remind you of all I told you.</span><br><span lang="ES">R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/14?21 ">John 14:21-26</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>Jesus said to his disciples:</span><br><span>“Whoever has my commandments and observes them</span><br><span>is the one who loves me.</span><br><span>Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father,</span><br><span>and I will love him and reveal myself to him.”</span><br><span>Judas, not the Iscariot, said to him,</span><br><span>“Master, then what happened that you will reveal yourself to us</span><br><span>and not to the world?”</span><br><span>Jesus answered and said to him,</span><br><span>“Whoever loves me will keep my word,</span><br><span>and my Father will love him,</span><br><span>and we will come to him and make our dwelling with him.</span><br><span>Whoever does not love me does not keep my words;</span><br><span>yet the word you hear is not mine</span><br><span>but that of the Father who sent me.</span><br><br><span>“I have told you this while I am with you.</span><br><span>The Advocate, the Holy Spirit</span><br><span>whom the Father will send in my name --</span><br><span>he will teach you everything</span><br><span>and remind you of all that I told you.”</span></p>
</div>


 - - -<br>

    </item>
<item>
  <title>Fifth Sunday of Easter</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/050326.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading 1  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/6?1 ">Acts 6:1-7</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>As the number of disciples continued to grow,<br>the Hellenists complained against the Hebrews<br>because their widows<br>were being neglected in the daily distribution.<br>So the Twelve called together the community of the disciples and said,<br>"It is not right for us to neglect the word of God to serve at table.<br>Brothers, select from among you seven reputable men,<br>filled with the Spirit and wisdom,<br>whom we shall appoint to this task,<br>whereas we shall devote ourselves to prayer<br>and to the ministry of the word."<br>The proposal was acceptable to the whole community,<br>so they chose Stephen, a man filled with faith and the Holy Spirit,<br>also Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas,<br>and Nicholas of Antioch, a convert to Judaism.<br>They presented these men to the apostles<br>who prayed and laid hands on them.<br>The word of God continued to spread,<br>and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem increased greatly;<br>even a large group of priests were becoming obedient to the faith.<br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/33?1 ">Psalm 33:1-2, 4-5, 18-19</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. (22) <strong>Lord, let your mercy be on us, as we place our trust in you.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>Exult, you just, in the LORD;<br>praise from the upright is fitting.<br>Give thanks to the LORD on the harp;<br>with the ten-stringed lyre chant his praises.<br>R. <strong>Lord, let your mercy be on us, as we place our trust in you.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>Upright is the word of the LORD,<br>and all his works are trustworthy.<br>He loves justice and right;<br>of the kindness of the LORD the earth is full.<br>R. <strong>Lord, let your mercy be on us, as we place our trust in you.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>See, the eyes of the LORD are upon those who fear him,<br>upon those who hope for his kindness,<br>To deliver them from death<br>and preserve them in spite of famine.<br>R. <strong>Lord, let your mercy be on us, as we place our trust in you.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Reading 2  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/1peter/2?4 ">1 Peter 2:4-9</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Beloved:<br>Come to him, a living stone, rejected by human beings<br>but chosen and precious in the sight of God,<br>and, like living stones,<br>let yourselves be built into a spiritual house<br>to be a holy priesthood to offer spiritual sacrifices<br>acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.<br>For it says in Scripture:<br><em>Behold, I am laying a stone in Zion,</em><br><em>a cornerstone, chosen and precious,</em><br><em>and whoever believes in it shall not be put to shame.</em><br>Therefore, its value is for you who have faith, but for those without faith:<br><em>The stone that the builders rejected</em><br><em>has become the cornerstone,</em> and<br><em>A stone that will make people stumble,</em><br><em>and a rock that will make them fall.</em><br>They stumble by disobeying the word, as is their destiny.<br><br>You are "a chosen race, a royal priesthood,<br>a holy nation, a people of his own,<br>so that you may announce the praises" of him<br>who called you out of darkness into his wonderful light.<br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/14?6 ">John 14:6</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>I am the way, the truth and the life, says the Lord;<br>no one comes to the Father, except through me.<br>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/14?1 ">John 14:1-12</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Jesus said to his disciples:<br>"Do not let your hearts be troubled.<br>You have faith in God; have faith also in me.<br>In my Father's house there are many dwelling places.<br>If there were not,<br>would I have told you that I am going to prepare a place for you?<br>And if I go and prepare a place for you,<br>I will come back again and take you to myself,<br>so that where I am you also may be.<br>Where I am going you know the way."<br>Thomas said to him,<br>"Master, we do not know where you are going;<br>how can we know the way?"<br>Jesus said to him, I am the way and the truth and the life.<br>No one comes to the Father except through me.<br>If you know me, then you will also know my Father.<br>From now on you do know him and have seen him."<br>Philip said to him,<br>"Master, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us."<br>Jesus said to him, "Have I been with you for so long a time<br>and you still do not know me, Philip?<br>Whoever has seen me has seen the Father.<br>How can you say, 'Show us the Father'?<br>Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me?<br>The words that I speak to you I do not speak on my own.<br>The Father who dwells in me is doing his works.<br>Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me,<br>or else, believe because of the works themselves.<br>Amen, amen, I say to you,<br>whoever believes in me will do the works that I do,<br>and will do greater ones than these,<br>because I am going to the Father."<br><br><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>


 - - -<br>

    </item>
<item>
  <title>Memorial of Saint Athanasius, Bishop and Doctor of the Church</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/050226.cfm</link>
  <description>


<ul class="nested"><li>Readings for the <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/0502-memorial-athanasius.cfm" class="item" hreflang="en">Memorial of Saint Athanasius, Bishop and Doctor of the Church</a></li></ul>
          <h4>Reading 1  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/13?44 ">Acts 13:44-52</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>On the following sabbath<br>almost the whole city<br>gathered to hear the word of the Lord.<br>When the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy<br>and with violent abuse contradicted what Paul said.<br>Both Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly and said,<br>“It was necessary that the word of God be spoken to you first,<br>but since you reject it<br>and condemn yourselves as unworthy of eternal life,<br>we now turn to the Gentiles.<br>For so the Lord has commanded us,<br><em>I have made you a light to the Gentiles,</em><br><em>that you may be an instrument of salvation</em><br><em>to the ends of the earth.”</em></p><p>The Gentiles were delighted when they heard this<br>and glorified the word of the Lord.<br>All who were destined for eternal life came to believe,<br>and the word of the Lord continued to spread<br>through the whole region.<br>The Jews, however, incited the women of prominence who were worshipers<br>and the leading men of the city,<br>stirred up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas,<br>and expelled them from their territory.<br>So they shook the dust from their feet in protest against them<br>and went to Iconium.<br>The disciples were filled with joy and the Holy Spirit.</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/98?1 ">Psalm 98:1, 2-3ab, 3cd-4</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;(3cd)&nbsp;<strong> All the ends of the earth have seen the saving power of God.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>Sing to the LORD a new song,<br>for he has done wondrous deeds;<br>His right hand has won victory for him,<br>his holy arm.<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>All the ends of the earth have seen the saving power of God.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>The LORD has made his salvation known:<br>in the sight of the nations he has revealed his justice.<br>He has remembered his kindness and his faithfulness<br>toward the house of Israel.<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>All the ends of the earth have seen the saving power of God.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>All the ends of the earth have seen<br>the salvation by our God.<br>Sing joyfully to the LORD, all you lands;<br>break into song; sing praise.<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>All the ends of the earth have seen the saving power of God.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/8?31 ">John 8:31b-32</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>If you remain in my word, you will truly be my disciples,<br>and you will know the truth, says the Lord.<br>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/14?7 ">John 14:7-14</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Jesus said to his disciples:<br>“If you know me, then you will also know my Father.<br>From now on you do know him and have seen him.”<br>Philip said to Jesus,<br>“Master, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us.”<br>Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you for so long a time<br>and you still do not know me, Philip?<br>Whoever has seen me has seen the Father.<br>How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?<br>Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me?<br>The words that I speak to you I do not speak on my own.<br>The Father who dwells in me is doing his works.<br>Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me,<br>or else, believe because of the works themselves.<br>Amen, amen, I say to you,<br>whoever believes in me will do the works that I do,<br>and will do greater ones than these,<br>because I am going to the Father.<br>And whatever you ask in my name, I will do,<br>so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.<br>If you ask anything of me in my name, I will do it.”</p>
</div>


 - - -<br>

    </item>
<item>
  <title>Friday of the Fourth Week of Easter</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/050126.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading I  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/13?26 ">Acts 13:26-33</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>When Paul came to Antioch in Pisidia, he said in the synagogue:</span><br><span>&nbsp;“My brothers, children of the family of Abraham,</span><br><span>and those others among you who are God-fearing,</span><br><span>to us this word of salvation has been sent.</span><br><span>The inhabitants of Jerusalem and their leaders failed to recognize him,</span><br><span>and by condemning him they fulfilled the oracles of the prophets</span><br><span>that are read sabbath after sabbath.</span><br><span>For even though they found no grounds for a death sentence,</span><br><span>they asked Pilate to have him put to death,</span><br><span>and when they had accomplished all that was written about him,</span><br><span>they took him down from the tree and placed him in a tomb.</span><br><span>But God raised him from the dead,</span><br><span>and for many days he appeared to those</span><br><span>who had come up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem.</span><br><span>These are now his witnesses before the people.</span><br><span>We ourselves are proclaiming this good news to you</span><br><span>that what God promised our fathers</span><br><span>he has brought to fulfillment for us, their children, by raising up Jesus,</span><br><span>as it is written in the second psalm,</span><br><em><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; You are my Son; this day I have begotten you.</span></em><span>”</span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/2?6 ">Psalm 2:6-7, 8-9, 10-11ab</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; (7bc)&nbsp; <strong>You are my Son; this day I have begotten you.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>“I myself have set up my king</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; on Zion, my holy mountain.”</span><br><span>I will proclaim the decree of the LORD:</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; The LORD said to me, “You are my Son;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; this day I have begotten you.”</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>You are my Son; this day I have begotten you.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>“Ask of me and I will give you</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; the nations for an inheritance</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; and the ends of the earth for your possession.</span><br><span>You shall rule them with an iron rod;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; you shall shatter them like an earthen dish.”</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>You are my Son; this day I have begotten you.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>And now, O kings, give heed;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; take warning, you rulers of the earth.</span><br><span>Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice before him;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; with trembling rejoice.</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>You are my Son; this day I have begotten you.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/14?6 ">John 14:6</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>I am the way and the truth and the life, says the Lord;</span><br><span>no one comes to the Father except through me.</span><br><span lang="ES">R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/14?1 ">John 14:1-6</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>Jesus said to his disciples:</span><br><span>“Do not let your hearts be troubled.</span><br><span>You have faith in God; have faith also in me.</span><br><span>In my Father’s house there are many dwelling places.</span><br><span>If there were not,</span><br><span>would I have told you that I am going to prepare a place for you?</span><br><span>And if I go and prepare a place for you,</span><br><span>I will come back again and take you to myself,</span><br><span>so that where I am you also may be.</span><br><span>Where I am going you know the way.”</span><br><span>Thomas said to him,</span><br><span>“Master, we do not know where you are going;</span><br><span>how can we know the way?”</span><br><span>Jesus said to him, “I am the way and the truth and the life.</span><br><span>No one comes to the Father except through me.”</span><br><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>

<ul class="nested"><li>Readings for the <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/0501-memorial-joseph-worker.cfm" class="item" hreflang="en">Optional Memorial of Saint Joseph the Worker</a></li></ul>
 - - -<br>
	<p>Lectionary for Mass for Use in the Dioceses of the United States, second typical edition, Copyright © 2001, 1998, 1997, 1986, 1970 Confraternity of Christian Doctrine; Psalm refrain © 1968, 1981, 1997, International Committee on English in the Liturgy, Inc. All rights reserved. Neither this work nor any part of it may be reproduced, distributed, performed or displayed in any medium, including electronic or digital, without permission in writing from the copyright owner.</p>

  <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 04:30:00 EDT</pubDate>
    <dc:creator>USCCB</dc:creator>
    <guid isPermaLink="true">https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/050126.cfm</guid>
    </item>
<item>
  <title>Thursday of the Fourth Week of Easter</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/043026.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading I  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/13?13 ">Acts 13:13-25</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>From Paphos, Paul and his companions</span><br><span>set sail and arrived at Perga in Pamphylia.</span><br><span>But John left them and returned to Jerusalem.</span><br><span>They continued on from Perga and reached Antioch in Pisidia.</span><br><span>On the sabbath they entered into the synagogue and took their seats.</span><br><span>After the reading of the law and the prophets,</span><br><span>the synagogue officials sent word to them,</span><br><span>“My brothers, if one of you has a word of exhortation</span><br><span>for the people, please speak.”</span><br><br><span>So Paul got up, motioned with his hand, and said,</span><br><span>“Fellow children of Israel and you others who are God-fearing, listen.</span><br><span>The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors</span><br><span>and exalted the people during their sojourn in the land of Egypt.</span><br><span>With uplifted arm he led them out,</span><br><span>and for about forty years he put up with them in the desert.</span><br><span>When he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan,</span><br><span>he gave them their land as an inheritance</span><br><span>at the end of about four hundred and fifty years.</span><br><span>After these things he provided judges up to Samuel the prophet.</span><br><span>Then they asked for a king.</span><br><span>God gave them Saul, son of Kish,</span><br><span>a man from the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.</span><br><span>Then he removed him and raised up David as their king;</span><br><span>of him he testified,</span><br><em><span>I have found David, son of Jesse, a man after my own heart;</span></em><br><em><span>he will carry out my every wish.</span></em><br><span>From this man’s descendants God, according to his promise,</span><br><span>has brought to Israel a savior, Jesus.</span><br><span>John heralded his coming by proclaiming a baptism of repentance</span><br><span>to all the people of Israel;</span><br><span>and as John was completing his course, he would say,</span><br><span>‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he.</span><br><span>Behold, one is coming after me;</span><br><span>I am not worthy to unfasten the sandals of his feet.’"</span><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/89?2 ">Psalm 89:2-3, 21-22, 25 and 27</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; (2)&nbsp; <strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>The favors of the LORD I will sing forever;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; through all generations my mouth shall proclaim your faithfulness.</span><br><span>For you have said, “My kindness is established forever”;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; in heaven you have confirmed your faithfulness.</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>“I have found David, my servant;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; with my holy oil I have anointed him,</span><br><span>That my hand may be always with him,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; and that my arm may make him strong.”</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>“My faithfulness and my mercy shall be with him,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; and through my name shall his horn be exalted.</span><br><span>He shall say of me, ‘You are my father,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; my God, the Rock, my savior.’”</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/revelation/1?5 ">See Revelation 1:5ab</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>Jesus Christ, you are the faithful witness,</span><br><span>the firstborn of the dead,</span><br><span>you have loved us and freed us from our sins by your Blood.</span><br><span lang="ES">R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/13?16 ">John 13:16-20</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>When Jesus had washed the disciples’ feet, he said to them:</span><br><span>“Amen, amen, I say to you, no slave is greater than his master</span><br><span>nor any messenger greater than the one who sent him.</span><br><span>If you understand this, blessed are you if you do it.</span><br><span>I am not speaking of all of you.</span><br><span>I know those whom I have chosen.</span><br><span>But so that the Scripture might be fulfilled,</span><br><em><span>The one who ate my food has raised his heel against me.</span></em><br><span>From now on I am telling you before it happens,</span><br><span>so that when it happens you may believe that I AM.</span><br><span>Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever receives the one I send</span><br><span>receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.”</span></p>
</div>

<ul class="nested"><li>Readings for the <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/0430-memorial-pius-v.cfm" class="item" hreflang="en">Optional Memorial of Saint Pius V, Pope, religious</a></li></ul>
 - - -<br>

  <pubDate>Thu, 30 Apr 2026 04:30:00 EDT</pubDate>
    <dc:creator>USCCB</dc:creator>
    <guid isPermaLink="true">https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/043026.cfm</guid>
    </item>
<item>
  <title>Memorial of Saint Catherine of Siena, Virgin and Doctor of the Church</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042926.cfm</link>
  <description>


<ul class="nested"><li>Readings for the <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/0429-memorial-catherine-siena.cfm" class="item" hreflang="en">Memorial of Saint Catherine of Siena, Virgin and Doctor of the Church</a></li></ul>
          <h4>Reading I <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/12?24 ">Acts 12:24—13:5a</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>The word of God continued to spread and grow.</span><br><br><span>After Barnabas and Saul completed their relief mission,</span><br><span>they returned to Jerusalem,</span><br><span>taking with them John, who is called Mark.</span><br><br><span>Now there were in the Church at Antioch prophets and teachers:</span><br><span>Barnabas, Symeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene,</span><br><span>Manaen who was a close friend of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.</span><br><span>While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said,</span><br><span>“Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul</span><br><span>for the work to which I have called them.”</span><br><span>Then, completing their fasting and prayer,</span><br><span>they laid hands on them and sent them off.</span><br><br><span>So they, sent forth by the Holy Spirit,</span><br><span>went down to Seleucia</span><br><span>and from there sailed to Cyprus.</span><br><span>When they arrived in Salamis,</span><br><span>they proclaimed the word of God in the Jewish synagogues.</span><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/67?2 ">Psalm 67:2-3, 5, 6 and 8</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; (4)&nbsp; <strong>O God, let all the nations praise you!</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>May God have pity on us and bless us;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; may he let his face shine upon us.</span><br><span>So may your way be known upon earth;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; among all nations, your salvation.</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>O God, let all the nations praise you!</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>May the nations be glad and exult</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; because you rule the peoples in equity;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; the nations on the earth you guide.</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>O God, let all the nations praise you!</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>May the peoples praise you, O God;</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; may all the peoples praise you!</span><br><span>May God bless us,</span><br><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; and may all the ends of the earth fear him!</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>O God, let all the nations praise you!</strong></span><br><span>or:</span><br><span>R.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Alleluia.</strong></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/8?12 ">John 8:12</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span><br><span>I am the light of the world, says the Lord;</span><br><span>whoever follows me will have the light of life.</span><br><span lang="ES">R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></span></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/12?44 ">John 12:44-50</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p><span>Jesus cried out and said,</span><br><span>“Whoever believes in me believes not only in me</span><br><span>but also in the one who sent me,</span><br><span>and whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.</span><br><span>I came into the world as light,</span><br><span>so that everyone who believes in me might not remain in darkness.</span><br><span>And if anyone hears my words and does not observe them,</span><br><span>I do not condemn him,</span><br><span>for I did not come to condemn the world but to save the world.</span><br><span>Whoever rejects me and does not accept my words</span><br><span>has something to judge him: the word that I spoke,</span><br><span>it will condemn him on the last day,</span><br><span>because I did not speak on my own,</span><br><span>but the Father who sent me commanded me what to say and speak.</span><br><span>And I know that his commandment is eternal life.</span><br><span>So what I say, I say as the Father told me.”</span></p>
</div>


 - - -<br>

  <pubDate>Wed, 29 Apr 2026 04:30:00 EDT</pubDate>
    <dc:creator>USCCB</dc:creator>
    <guid isPermaLink="true">https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042926.cfm</guid>
    </item>
<item>
  <title>Tuesday of the Fourth Week of Easter</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042826.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading 1  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/11?19 ">Acts 11:19-26</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Those who had been scattered by the persecution<br>that arose because of Stephen<br>went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch,<br>preaching the word to no one but Jews.<br>There were some Cypriots and Cyrenians among them, however,<br>who came to Antioch and began to speak to the Greeks as well,<br>proclaiming the Lord Jesus.<br>The hand of the Lord was with them<br>and a great number who believed turned to the Lord.<br>The news about them reached the ears of the Church in Jerusalem,<br>and they sent Barnabas to go to Antioch.<br>When he arrived and saw the grace of God,<br>he rejoiced and encouraged them all<br>to remain faithful to the Lord in firmness of heart,<br>for he was a good man, filled with the Holy Spirit and faith.<br>And a large number of people was added to the Lord.<br>Then he went to Tarsus to look for Saul,<br>and when he had found him he brought him to Antioch.<br>For a whole year they met with the Church<br>and taught a large number of people,<br>and it was in Antioch that the disciples<br>were first called Christians.</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/87?1 ">Psalm 87:1b-3, 4-5, 6-7</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;(117:1a)&nbsp; <strong>All you nations, praise the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>His foundation upon the holy mountains<br>the LORD loves:<br>The gates of Zion,<br>more than any dwelling of Jacob.<br>Glorious things are said of you,<br>O city of God!<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>All you nations, praise the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia</strong>.<br>I tell of Egypt and Babylon<br>among those who know the LORD;<br>Of Philistia, Tyre, Ethiopia:<br>“This man was born there.”<br>And of Zion they shall say:<br>“One and all were born in her;<br>And he who has established her<br>is the Most High LORD.”<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>All you nations, praise the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>They shall note, when the peoples are enrolled:<br>“This man was born there.”<br>And all shall sing, in their festive dance:<br>“My home is within you.”<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>All you nations, praise the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/10?27 ">John 10:27</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>My sheep hear my voice, says the Lord;<br>I know them, and they follow me.<br>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/10?22 ">John 10:22-30</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>The feast of the Dedication was taking place in Jerusalem.<br>It was winter.<br>And Jesus walked about in the temple area on the Portico of Solomon.<br>So the Jews gathered around him and said to him,<br>“How long are you going to keep us in suspense?<br>If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”<br>Jesus answered them, “I told you and you do not believe.<br>The works I do in my Father’s name testify to me.<br>But you do not believe, because you are not among my sheep.<br>My sheep hear my voice;<br>I know them, and they follow me.<br>I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish.<br>No one can take them out of my hand.<br>My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all,<br>and no one can take them out of the Father’s hand.<br>The Father and I are one.”</p>
</div>

<ul class="nested"><li>Readings for the <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/0428-memorial-louis-de-montfort.cfm" class="item" hreflang="en">Optional Memorial of Saint Louis Mary de Montfort, Priest</a></li><li>Readings for the <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/0428-memorial-peter-chanel.cfm" class="item" hreflang="en">Optional Memorial of Saint Peter Chanel, Priest and Martyr</a></li></ul>

Neither this work nor any part of it may be reproduced, distributed, performed or displayed in any medium, including electronic or digital, without permission in writing from the copyright owner.</p>

  <pubDate>Tue, 28 Apr 2026 04:30:00 EDT</pubDate>
    <dc:creator>USCCB</dc:creator>
    <guid isPermaLink="true">https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042826.cfm</guid>
    </item>
<item>
  <title>Monday of the Fourth Week of Easter</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042726.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading 1  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/11?1 ">Acts 11:1-18</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>The Apostles and the brothers who were in Judea<br>heard that the Gentiles too had accepted the word of God.<br>So when Peter went up to Jerusalem<br>the circumcised believers confronted him, saying,<br>‘You entered the house of uncircumcised people and ate with them.”<br>Peter began and explained it to them step by step, saying,<br>“I was at prayer in the city of Joppa<br>when in a trance I had a vision,<br>something resembling a large sheet coming down,<br>lowered from the sky by its four corners, and it came to me.<br>Looking intently into it,<br>I observed and saw the four-legged animals of the earth,<br>the wild beasts, the reptiles, and the birds of the sky.<br>I also heard a voice say to me, ‘Get up, Peter. Slaughter and eat.’<br>But I said, ‘Certainly not, sir,<br>because nothing profane or unclean has ever entered my mouth.’<br>But a second time a voice from heaven answered,<br>‘What God has made clean, you are not to call profane.’<br>This happened three times,<br>and then everything was drawn up again into the sky.<br>Just then three men appeared at the house where we were,<br>who had been sent to me from Caesarea.<br>The Spirit told me to accompany them without discriminating.<br>These six brothers also went with me,<br>and we entered the man’s house.<br>He related to us how he had seen the angel standing in his house, saying,<br>‘Send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter,<br>who will speak words to you<br>by which you and all your household will be saved.’<br>As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell upon them<br>as it had upon us at the beginning,<br>and I remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said,<br>‘John baptized with water<br>but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.’<br>If then God gave them the same gift he gave to us<br>when we came to believe in the Lord Jesus Christ,<br>who was I to be able to hinder God?”<br>When they heard this,<br>they stopped objecting and glorified God, saying,<br>“God has then granted life-giving repentance to the Gentiles too.”</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/42?2 ">Psalm 42:2-3; 43:3, 4</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. (see 3a) <strong>Athirst is my soul for the living God.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>As the hind longs for the running waters,<br>so my soul longs for you, O God.<br>Athirst is my soul for God, the living God.<br>When shall I go and behold the face of God?<br>R. <strong>Athirst is my soul for the living God.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>Send forth your light and your fidelity;<br>they shall lead me on<br>And bring me to your holy mountain,<br>to your dwelling-place.<br>R. <strong>Athirst is my soul for the living God.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>Then will I go in to the altar of God,<br>the God of my gladness and joy;<br>Then will I give you thanks upon the harp,<br>O God, my God!<br>R. <strong>Athirst is my soul for the living God.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/10?14 ">John 10:14</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>I am the good shepherd, says the Lord;<br>I know my sheep, and mine know me.<br>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/10?11 ">John 10:11-18</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Jesus said:<br>“I am the good shepherd.<br>A good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.<br>A hired man, who is not a shepherd<br>and whose sheep are not his own,<br>sees a wolf coming and leaves the sheep and runs away,<br>and the wolf catches and scatters them.<br>This is because he works for pay and has no concern for the sheep.<br>I am the good shepherd,<br>and I know mine and mine know me,<br>just as the Father knows me and I know the Father;<br>and I will lay down my life for the sheep.<br>I have other sheep that do not belong to this fold.<br>These also I must lead, and they will hear my voice,<br>and there will be one flock, one shepherd.<br>This is why the Father loves me,<br>because I lay down my life in order to take it up again.<br>No one takes it from me, but I lay it down on my own.<br>I have power to lay it down, and power to take it up again.<br>This command I have received from my Father.”</p>
</div>


 - - -<br>

  <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 04:30:00 EDT</pubDate>
    <dc:creator>USCCB</dc:creator>
    <guid isPermaLink="true">https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042726.cfm</guid>
    </item>
<item>
  <title>Fourth Sunday of Easter</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042626.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading 1  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/acts/2?14 ">Acts 2:14a, 36-41</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Then Peter stood up with the Eleven,<br>raised his voice, and proclaimed:<br>"Let the whole house of Israel know for certain<br>that God has made both Lord and Christ,<br>this Jesus whom you crucified."<br><br>Now when they heard this, they were cut to the heart,<br>and they asked Peter and the other apostles,<br>"What are we to do, my brothers?"<br>Peter said to them,<br>"Repent and be baptized, every one of you,<br>in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins;<br>and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.<br>For the promise is made to you and to your children<br>and to all those far off,<br>whomever the Lord our God will call."<br>He testified with many other arguments, and was exhorting them,<br>"Save yourselves from this corrupt generation."<br>Those who accepted his message were baptized,<br>and about three thousand persons were added that day.<br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/23?1 ">Psalm 23: 1-3a, 3b4, 5, 6</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. (1) <strong>The Lord is my shepherd; there is nothing I shall want.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.<br>In verdant pastures he gives me repose;<br>beside restful waters he leads me;<br>he refreshes my soul.<br>R. <strong>The Lord is my shepherd; there is nothing I shall want.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>He guides me in right paths<br>for his name's sake.<br>Even though I walk in the dark valley<br>I fear no evil; for you are at my side.<br>With your rod and your staff<br>that give me courage.<br>R. <strong>The Lord is my shepherd; there is nothing I shall want.</strong><br>or:<br>R.<strong> Alleluia.</strong><br>You spread the table before me<br>in the sight of my foes;<br>you anoint my head with oil;<br>my cup overflows.&nbsp;<br>R. <strong>The Lord is my shepherd; there is nothing I shall want.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>Only goodness and kindness follow me<br>all the days of my life;<br>and I shall dwell in the house of the LORD<br>for years to come.<br>R. <strong>The Lord is my shepherd; there is nothing I shall want.</strong><br>or:<br>R. <strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Reading 2  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/1peter/2?20 ">1 Peter 2:20b-25</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Beloved:<br>If you are patient when you suffer for doing what is good,<br>this is a grace before God.<br>For to this you have been called,<br>because Christ also suffered for you,<br>leaving you an example that you should follow in his footsteps.<br><em>He committed no sin, and no deceit was found in his mouth.</em><br><br>When he was insulted, he returned no insult;<br>when he suffered, he did not threaten;<br>instead, he handed himself over to the one who judges justly.<br>He himself bore our sins in his body upon the cross,<br>so that, free from sin, we might live for righteousness.<br>By his wounds you have been healed.<br>For you had gone astray like sheep,<br>but you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your souls.<br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/10?14 ">John 10:14</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>I am the good shepherd, says the Lord;<br>I know my sheep, and mine know me.<br>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/john/10?1 ">John 10:1-10</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Jesus said:<br>"Amen, amen, I say to you,<br>whoever does not enter a sheepfold through the gate<br>but climbs over elsewhere is a thief and a robber.<br>But whoever enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.<br>The gatekeeper opens it for him, and the sheep hear his voice,<br>as the shepherd calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.<br>When he has driven out all his own,<br>he walks ahead of them, and the sheep follow him,<br>because they recognize his voice.<br>But they will not follow a stranger;<br>they will run away from him,<br>because they do not recognize the voice of strangers."<br>Although Jesus used this figure of speech,<br>the Pharisees did not realize what he was trying to tell them.<br><br>So Jesus said again, "Amen, amen, I say to you,<br>I am the gate for the sheep.<br>All who came before me are thieves and robbers,<br>but the sheep did not listen to them.<br>I am the gate.<br>Whoever enters through me will be saved,<br>and will come in and go out and find pasture.<br>A thief comes only to steal and slaughter and destroy;<br>I came so that they might have life and have it more abundantly."<br><br><br>&nbsp;</p>
</div>


 - - -<br>

    </item>
<item>
  <title>Feast of Saint Mark, evangelist</title>
  <link>https://bible.usccb.org/bible/readings/042526.cfm</link>
  <description>



          <h4>Reading 1  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/1peter/5?5 ">1 Peter 5:5b-14</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Beloved:&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<br>Clothe yourselves with humility<br>in your dealings with one another, for:</p><p><em>God opposes the proud</em><br><em>but bestows favor on the humble.</em></p><p>So humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God,<br>that he may exalt you in due time.<br>Cast all your worries upon him because he cares for you.</p><p>Be sober and vigilant.<br>Your opponent the Devil is prowling around like a roaring lion<br>looking for someone to devour.<br>Resist him, steadfast in faith,<br>knowing that your brothers and sisters throughout the world<br>undergo the same sufferings.<br>The God of all grace<br>who called you to his eternal glory through Christ Jesus<br>will himself restore, confirm, strengthen, and establish you<br>after you have suffered a little.<br>To him be dominion forever.&nbsp; Amen.</p><p>I write you this briefly through Silvanus,<br>whom I consider a faithful brother,<br>exhorting you and testifying that this is the true grace of God.<br>Remain firm in it.<br>The chosen one at Babylon sends you greeting, as does Mark, my son.<br>Greet one another with a loving kiss.<br>Peace to all of you who are in Christ.</p>
</div>
        <h4>Responsorial Psalm  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/psalms/89?2 ">Psalm 89:2-3, 6-7, 16-17</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;(2)&nbsp; <strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>The favors of the LORD I will sing forever;<br>through all generations my mouth shall proclaim your faithfulness.<br>For you have said, “My kindness is established forever”;<br>in heaven you have confirmed your faithfulness.<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>The heavens proclaim your wonders, O LORD,<br>and your faithfulness, in the assembly of the holy ones.<br>For who in the skies can rank with the LORD?<br>Who is like the LORD among the sons of God?<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong><br>Blessed the people who know the joyful shout;<br>in the light of your countenance, O LORD, they walk.<br>At your name they rejoice all the day,<br>and through your justice they are exalted.<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>For ever I will sing the goodness of the Lord.</strong><br>or:<br>R.&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;<strong>Alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Alleluia  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/1corinthians/1?23 ">1 Corinthians 1:23a-24b</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong><br>We proclaim Christ crucified;<br>he is the power of God and the wisdom of God.<br>R. <strong>Alleluia, alleluia.</strong></p>
</div>
        <h4>Gospel  <a href="https://bible.usccb.org/bible/mark/16?15 ">Mark 16:15-20</a></h4> 
<div class="poetry"> 
  <p>Jesus appeared to the Eleven and said to them:<br>“Go into the whole world<br>and proclaim the Gospel to every creature.<br>Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved;<br>whoever does not believe will be condemned.<br>These signs will accompany those who believe:<br>in my name they will drive out demons,<br>they will speak new languages.<br>They will pick up serpents with their hands,<br>and if they drink any deadly thing, it will not harm them.<br>They will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.”</p><p>Then the Lord Jesus, after he spoke to them,<br>was taken up into heaven<br>and took his seat at the right hand of God.<br>But they went forth and preached everywhere,<br>while the Lord worked with them<br>and confirmed the word through accompanying signs.</p>
</div>
